frameworks.name: signal-detection theory (unequal-variance)
frameworks.one_line: UV-SDT extended by using response time instead of confidence to estimate variance asymmetry.

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory (equal-variance)
frameworks.one_line: Canonical EV-SDT critiqued for overestimating detection sensitivity.

frameworks.name: confidence-based SDT
frameworks.one_line: Traditional confidence-rating method used as a benchmark for RT-based estimates.

core_question: Can response time data be used to estimate unequal-variance SDT parameters in perceptual detection tasks?
key_assumptions: Type-1 ROC asymmetry reflects greater variability in stimulus-present trials
RT distributions carry information about underlying signal variance
Detection tasks involve asymmetry between signal-present and signal-absent trials
manipulations: SDT model variant: equal- vs unequal-variance
key_findings: RT-based estimates of SD ratio (σ) and mean difference (μ) align closely with confidence-based estimates
Sensitivity measure dₐ from RT and from confidence shows strong consistency across datasets
Conventional d′ consistently overestimates detection performance relative to dₐ
interpretations: Response time provides a valid alternative route to unequal-variance SDT parameters
Equal-variance assumption inflates apparent sensitivity in detection tasks
